volumeTitle: The Merchant. The Braggart Soldier. The Ghost. The Persian
label: The Ghost (Section 3.1.641b - Section 3.2.723b)

sections.label: Section 3.1.641b
sections.translation: Out with you !

sections.label: Section 3.1.641c
sections.translation: What ’ s the matter ?

sections.label: Section 3.1.641d
sections.translation: Don ’ t ask me that .

sections.label: Section 3.1.642
sections.translation: But why so ?

sections.label: Section 3.1.642b
sections.translation: Bright as a mirror , pure brilliancy itself .

sections.label: Section 3.1.643
sections.original: Bene hercle factum . quid , eas quanti destinat ?
sections.translation: Excellently done , upon my faith ! Well , how much did he agree to give for it ?

sections.label: Section 3.1.644
sections.original: Talentis magnis totidem quot ego et tu sumus .
sections.translation: As many great talents as you and I put together make ;

sections.label: Section 3.1.645
sections.original: sed arraboni has dedit quadraginta minas ;
sections.translation: but these forty minae he paid by way of earnest . ( Pointing to the BANKER .) From him he received what we paid the other man . Do you quite understand [*] ? For after this house was in such a state as I mentioned to you , he at once purchased another house for himself .

sections.label: Section 3.1.649_651
sections.translation: Excellently done , upon my faith !

sections.label: Section 3.1.649_651b
sections.translation: Hark you . Mid-day is now close at hand .

sections.label: Section 3.1.652
sections.original: Absolve hunc quaeso , vomitu ne hic nos enecet .
sections.translation: Prithee , do dismiss this puking fellow , that he mayn ’ t worry us to death .

sections.label: Section 3.1.653
sections.translation: Young man , transact the business with me .

sections.label: Section 3.1.653b
sections.translation: I ’ m to ask it of you , you mean ?

sections.label: Section 3.1.654
sections.translation: Come for it to-morrow .

sections.label: Section 3.1.654b
sections.original: Abeo : sat habeo , si cras fero .—
sections.translation: I ’ ll be off , then ; I ’ m quite satisfied if I get it tomorrow .

sections.label: Section 3.1.655
sections.original: Malum quod isti di deaeque omnes duint ,
sections.translation: A plague may all the Gods and Goddesses send upon him ! so utterly has he disarranged my plans . On my word , no class of men is there more disgusting , or less acquainted with fair dealing than the banking race .

sections.label: Section 3.1.659
sections.original: Qua in regione istas aedis emit filius ?
sections.translation: In what neighbourhood did my son buy this house ?

sections.label: Section 3.1.660
sections.translation: Just see that , now ! I ’ m undone !

sections.label: Section 3.1.660b
sections.translation: Are you going to tell me that which I ask you ?

sections.label: Section 3.1.661
sections.original: Dicam . sed nomen domini quaero quid siet .
sections.translation: I ’ ll tell you ; but I ’ m thinking what was the name of the owner .

sections.label: Section 3.1.662
sections.translation: Well , call it to mind , then .

sections.label: Section 3.1.662b
sections.translation: What am I to do now , except put the lie upon this neighbour of ours next door ? I ’ ll say that his son has bought that house .

sections.label: Section 3.1.665
sections.original: calidum hercle esse audivi optimum mendacium .
sections.translation: I ’ faith , I ’ ve heard say that a lie piping-hot is the best lie ; this is piping-hot ; although it is at a distance off , it scorches badly . Whatever the Gods dictate , that am I determined to say .

sections.label: Section 3.1.667
sections.translation: Well now ? Have you recollected it by this ?

sections.label: Section 3.1.667b
sections.translation: May the Gods confound that fellow !— no this other fellow , rather .

sections.label: Section 3.1.669_670
sections.translation: ( To THEUROPIDES .) Your son has bought the house of this next-door neighbour of yours .

sections.label: Section 3.1.669_670b
sections.translation: In real truth ?

sections.label: Section 3.1.671
sections.translation: If , indeed , you are going to pay down the money , then in real truth ; if you are not going to pay it , in real truth he has not bought it .

sections.label: Section 3.1.673
sections.translation: He hasn ’ t bought it in a very good situation .

sections.label: Section 3.1.673b
sections.translation: Why yes , in a very good one .

sections.label: Section 3.1.674
sections.original: cupio hercle inspicere hasce aedis . pultadum fores
sections.translation: I ’ faith , I should like to look over this house ; just knock at the door , and call some one to you from within , Tranio .

sections.label: Section 3.1.676
sections.original: Ecce autem perii . nunc quid dicam nescio .
sections.translation: Why just look now , again I don ’ t know what I ’ m to say . Once more , now , are the surges bearing me upon the self-same rock . What now ? I ’ faith , I can ’ t discover what I am now to do ; I ’ m caught in the fact .

sections.label: Section 3.1.679b
sections.translation: Just call some one out of doors ;

sections.label: Section 3.1.680
sections.translation: ask him to show us round .

sections.label: Section 3.1.680b
sections.original: Heus tu , at hic sunt mulieres :
sections.translation: Hallo there , you ! ( Turning round ) But there are ladies here ; we must first see whether they are willing or unwilling .

sections.label: Section 3.1.682
sections.original: Bonum aequomque oras . i , percontare et roga .
sections.translation: You say what ’ s good and proper ; just make enquiry , and ask . I ’ ll wait here outside until you come out .

sections.label: Section 3.1.684
sections.original: Di te deaeque ómnes funditus perdant , senex ,
sections.translation: May all the Gods and Goddesses utterly confound you , old gentleman !

sections.label: Section 3.1.685
sections.original: itá mea consilia undique oppugnas male .
sections.translation: in such a fashion are you thwarting my artful plans in every way . Bravo ! very good ! Look , Simo himself , the owner of the house , is coming out of doors . I ’ ll step aside here , until I have convened the senate of council in my mind . Then , when I ’ ve discovered what I am to do , I ’ ll join him . ( THEUROPIDES and TRANIO stand at a distance from SIMO ’ s house , in opposite directions , THEUROPIDES being out of sight .)

sections.label: Section 3.2.690
sections.original: Mélius anno hóc mihi nón fuit domi ,
sections.translation: I ’ ve not enjoyed myself better at home this year than I have to-day , nor has at any time any meal pleased me better . My wife provided a very nice breakfast for me ; now she bids me go take a nap . By no means ! It instantly struck me that it didn ’ t so happen by chance .

sections.label: Section 3.2.695
sections.original: mélius quom prándium quám solet dedit :
sections.translation: She provided a better breakfast than is her wont ; and then , the old lady wanted to draw me away to my chamber . Sleep is not good [*] after breakfast — out upon it ! I secretly stole away from the house , out of doors . My wife , I ’ m sure , is now quite bursting with rage at home .

sections.label: Section 3.2.700
sections.original: Rés parata ést mala in vésperum huic seni .
sections.translation: A sore mischance is provided for this old fellow by the evening ; for he must both dine and go to bed in-doors in sorry fashion .

sections.label: Section 3.2.702
sections.original: Quóm magis cógito cúm meo animo :
sections.translation: The more I reflect upon it in my mind : if any person has a dowried wife , sleep has no charms for him .

sections.label: Section 3.2.705
sections.original: íre dormítum odio est véluti nunc mihi
sections.translation: I detest going to take a nap . It ’ s a settled matter with me to be off to the Forum from here , rather than nap it at home . And , i ’ faith ( to the AUDIENCE ) , I don ’ t know how your wives are in their behaviour ; this wife of mine , I know right well how badly she treats me ,

sections.label: Section 3.2.710
sections.original: péius posthác fore quám fuit mihi .
sections.translation: and that she will prove more annoying to me hereafter than she has been .

sections.label: Section 3.2.711
sections.original: Ábitus tuos tíbi , senex , fécerit male :
sections.translation: If your escape , old gentleman , turns out amiss , there ’ ll be no reason for you to be accusing any one of the Gods ; by very good right , you may justly lay the blame upon yourself . It ’ s time now for me to accost this old fellow .

sections.label: Section 3.2.715
sections.original: hóc habet . répperi quí senem dúcerem ,
sections.translation: ’ Tis down upon him [*] . I ’ ve hit upon a plan whereby to cajole the old fellow , by means of which to drive grief [*] away from me . I ’ ll accost him . ( Accosting him .) May the Gods , Simo , send on you many blessings !

sections.label: Section 3.2.718
sections.translation: Save you , Tranio !

sections.label: Section 3.2.718b
sections.translation: How fare you ?

sections.label: Section 3.2.718c
sections.translation: Not amiss . What are you about ?

sections.label: Section 3.2.719b
sections.translation: Holding by the hand a very worthy man .

sections.label: Section 3.2.719c
sections.translation: You act in a friendly way ,

sections.label: Section 3.2.720
sections.translation: in speaking well of me .

sections.label: Section 3.2.720b
sections.translation: It certainly is your due .

sections.label: Section 3.2.720c
sections.translation: But , i ’ faith , in you I don ’ t hold a good servant by the hand .

sections.label: Section 3.2.721a
sections.translation: Hark you ! you whip-knave , come back to me .

sections.label: Section 3.2.721ab
sections.translation: I ’ ll be there just now .

sections.label: Section 3.2.722
sections.translation: Well now , how soon —?

sections.label: Section 3.2.722b
sections.translation: What is it ?

sections.label: Section 3.2.722c
sections.translation: The usual goings-on .

sections.label: Section 3.2.723b
sections.translation: Tell me then , these usual goings-on , what are they ?
